cluster: C4 store-signals — "Fail closed, sweep safe": signal-target validation fail-closed semantics (signal.c), crash-safe record temps + two-phase-aware purge sweep (record.c/list.c/shell.c), purge deletion targets derived from (layout, id) instead of record-stored path strings (list.c). Baseline verified: v0.6.1, branch tempo-refinement, 135/135 tests, all dossier line citations re-read and confirmed accurate except where noted in verdicts.

play_verdicts.play: Signal validation fails closed (leader-gone starttime floor + exe-read failure = unverified + SPEC.md:59-60 narrowing)
play_verdicts.reason: Core finding is code-true: signal.c:112 computes have_identity_token as mere NONZERO-in-record, and the two authorize paths (signal.c:137-142 live-leader, :151-156 leader-gone GROUP_LIVE) check presence, never comparison; signal.c:121-129 rejects only on (read==0 && mismatch), so a -1 from hold_read_proc_exe silently passes and line 138 converts verification failure into success. The error string at :139 does narrate the design bug as claimed. BUT the play's assumption #2 is FALSE: tests/test_hold.sh:1873-1881 (test_leader_zombie_group_still_running, registered :4170) pins the leader-gone GROUP_LIVE fallback SUCCEEDING with an uncompared token — 'hold -d bash -c "sleep 60 & exit 0"' then 'hold end' must exit 0. This kills the finding's alternative remedy ('refuse leaderless groups') outright; only the min-starttime-floor variant is admissible, and it happens to keep :1873 green because the surviving 'sleep 60' starts at/after the leader. Sketch item (3) (re-read leader stat as last act before each kill) is rejected as theater: validate_signal_target already runs at :250 and :286 immediately before the kills at :263/:298; the window is irreducible (the finding at signal.c:263 admits it) and a duplicate read is pure ballooning. The play's own strongest_objection is correct and binding: the floor does NOT close the pid-wrap attack (wrapped members have LATER starttimes), so the SPEC.md:59-60 rewording must land in the same commit or the hardening is cosmetic. Also verified: the TERM-then-KILL escalation branch (signal.c:279-312, STOP_TIMEOUT_MS=5000 at config.h:51, 1000ms KILL wait at :306) is exercised by ZERO existing tests — no TERM-trapping child exists anywhere in tests/ (grep 'trap' finds only the suite EXIT traps) — so there is no timing pin to break, and the cluster must add one.
amendments: Drop sketch item (3) entirely (no re-read before kill); ship items (1),(2),(4),(5) as one commit — hardening and SPEC honesty are inseparable per the play's own strongest_objection.

Pin exact fallback semantics: leader-gone + GROUP_LIVE + starttime token -> authorize only when a live-member scan finds >=1 non-zombie member AND min live-member starttime >= r->proc_starttime_ticks (strict < refuses; equal ticks pass — children forked in the same tick; skip 'Z' members exactly like hold_group_session_liveness at process.c:179).

Leader-gone + exe-only token (proc_starttime_ticks==0) must REFUSE rc 2: an exe token cannot be compared against a dead leader, and members legitimately exec different binaries. This is a semantic tightening beyond the sketch; it is safe because both record writers (start.c:1389, shell.c:558) essentially always capture starttime — starttime-0 records are degenerate. No test pins the exe-only leader-gone path (verified by grep).

New refusal messages must contain the phrase 'cannot be signaled' — test_hold.sh:788 greps the family 'process group/session differs|cannot be signaled'; use 'hold: error: call %s process identity is unverifiable and cannotot be signaled' shape (spelled correctly) to stay in idiom.

The HOLD_TEST_FAIL_PROC_EXE hook must be #ifdef HOLD_TESTING-guarded at the signal.c call site (layout.c:111-116 is the guarded exemplar; start.c:1406/:1441 are the UNGUARDED anti-pattern the ledger separately faults — do not copy it). Makefile:37 confirms the test binary builds -DHOLD_TESTING.

New helper hold_group_min_live_member_starttime(pgid, sid, uint64_t *min_out) lives in src/platform/process.c with BOTH branches: Linux /proc walk reusing read_process_ids_state (strict parse_pid_token parser, process.c:29-38) + hold_read_proc_stat_tokens; __APPLE__ KERN_PROC_ALL walk reading kp_proc.p_starttime in usec (same units process.c:260-265 records). Declare in include/hold/platform.h; layer direction is clean (platform < runtime per scripts/lint_layers.sh).

Do NOT touch the matching fail-open in state.c:54-59 in this commit — it drives list/ps display and stale-purge semantics with a wide test blast radius; carve as named deferral 'state.c exe fail-open' (see implementation_risks).

SPEC.md:59-60 exact rewording: 'Signals are delivered to the group only after the recorded identity (start time, exe identity) revalidates immediately before every delivery — a recycled PID is never knowingly signaled.' docs/identity.md:98 already states the refusal doctrine and needs no change.

play_verdicts.play: Store lifecycle: unique temps for record/public writers, sweep honors .reserve, .log.idx collection, corrupt-record removals reported
play_verdicts.reason: The two FACT findings re-verified live in code: record.c:156/:163 opens fixed-name '.%s.tmp' with O_EXCL and unlinks it only on its own failure (:384-386) — a SIGKILL mid-write bricks every future rewrite (rename dies via hold_die_errno at call.c 'failed to write renamed call record'; the reaper's 50x100ms loop at start.c:1111-1117 exhausts silently). hold_open_unique_temp (fs.c:60-85, declared core.h:28) has zero callers — confirmed by grep — so the fix revives a dead export instead of adding machinery. Sweep gaps confirmed: list.c:748-751/:784-786 unlink orphan logs/socks checking only runs/<id>.json absence, never the .<id>.reserve that layout.c:169 (id_collides_in_store) and start.c:155 (run_id_material_exists) already treat as 'id taken' — start holds the reserve across exactly the log-before-JSON window (reserve at start.c:893/:915, log preflight :924-932, JSON at :1408). The idx orphan is real but the dossier's framing needs one correction: the orphan-log pass at :748-750 ALREADY calls unlink_log_index_for_log — the actual hole is the record-branch at :700-703 (no idx unlink) combined with '.log.idx' failing the '.log' suffix match at :724 once the record branch has removed the .log; the one-call fix stands. Corrupt-record silent unlink confirmed at :681-688 vs list's warning at :201-203, violating SPEC.md:61-62. Two holes in the play as sketched: (1) reserve-skip with no staleness rule turns a crashed start (SIGKILL leaves the reserve — only error paths unlink it, start.c:905-1050) into a PERMANENT sweep blind spot for that id's log/sock; (2) the adopted-call path (shell.c) creates log + console sock with NO reserve at all — shell_id_available (shell.c:318-340) never checks one and adopt never creates one — so the exact race the play claims to close stays open for 'hold on' adoptions, contradicting the play's own uniformity thesis. The unique-temp objection (silent last-write-wins replaces loud EEXIST) is honest and acceptable only with the flock fix as a NAMED deferral.
amendments: Reserve-skip must be age-gated: skip the orphan log/sock only when record_dir/.<id>.reserve exists AND its mtime is younger than 600s; an older reserve is a dead start's litter — unlink the reserve and proceed. (start's reserve->JSON window is sub-second; 600s is generous.) This keeps test_hold.sh:719-725 (test_orphan_log_cleanup, reg :4108) green: no reserve planted there.

Extend two-phase creation to the adopted path: in shell.c adopt_foreground_group, create .<id>.reserve (O_EXCL, mirroring reserve_hashed_run_id start.c:208-214) immediately after shell_hashed_adopt_id, before the log/sock creation at shell.c:469/:481; add the reserve check to shell_id_available; unlink it on every adopt failure path (record write already unlinks it — record.c:365 fires on ALL record writes). Without this, the play's thesis is half-true.

Corrupt-record accounting must NOT inflate stats->removed: the sweep prints one stdout line per purged call (list.c:711, 'Docker prune prints what it deletes') and the summary says 'purged N past calls' (:863) — folding corrupt files in makes count and lines disagree and the summary lie. Use a separate removed_corrupt counter, stderr line 'hold: warning: removing corrupt record %s' per file, summary suffix '; removed N corrupt record(s)', and print 'nothing to purge' only when both counters are 0. No test greps the summary strings today (verified), so wording is free — but stdout must stay machine-only per docker-parity-contract.md:66-69.

Keep the fchmod(fd, 0644) at record.c:405 when switching the public writer to hold_open_unique_temp — the mode argument is umask-subject exactly like the current open; dropping the fchmod would silently narrow public-entry permissions under restrictive umasks.

.tmp litter pass: in the record-dir and public-dir sweeps, collect dot-prefixed names ending '.tmp' only when mtime is older than 60s (protects a live writer's sub-ms window; unlinking a live tmp would make its rename fail loudly, but don't create that noise). The same rule collects legacy fixed-name .<id>.tmp tombs from existing v0.6.1 stores. Do NOT parse the embedded pid (the sketch's pid-liveness idea) — mtime is simpler and pid-in-name checking adds a parser for no gain.

NAMED DEFERRAL 'record-write serialization': per-id flock for load-modify-write (record.c:1015 rename/save vs reaper mark-finished lost updates). Unique temps convert today's loud EEXIST collision into silent last-write-wins; the commit message must state this trade explicitly. Do not attempt flock in this commit.
files_touched must add src/runtime/shell.c (adopted reserve); include/hold/core.h needs no change (declaration exists).

play_verdicts.play: Purge derives unlink targets from (store layout, call id); record path strings never followed for deletion
play_verdicts.reason: Verified end-to-end: the only two destructive consumers of record-stored paths are hold_prune_one_run (list.c:640-646) and the sweep record-branch (:700-706) — a full grep of unlink() sites shows every other unlink operates on paths the code itself constructed (start.c rollback paths, broker/socket own-path cleanup, computed orphan sweeps). hold_valid_record (validate.c:82-85) constrains nothing about paths; load accepts log_path verbatim (record.c:624-628) and console_sock with only a leading-'/' check (:677-679). Every writer constructs from layout: start.c:911 ('%s/%s.log') and :920 (hold_format_console_sock_path), shell.c:469/:481 identically, redial reuses the originally-constructed path (start.c:875-885); no --log-path flag exists (grep of cli_main.c returns nothing). So derivation is behavior-identical for every record hold ever wrote, and it removes the vulnerability class instead of adding a confinement check — genuinely stronger than the finding's own remedy_hint (prefix validation). The migration objection is actually WEAKER than stated: after a store move, today's code unlinks the stale stored path (ENOENT no-op) and LEAKS the real store-resident log until the next full sweep; derivation cleans the store-resident file and warns about the divergent stored path — a net improvement, not a regression. Two claims corrected: 'net negative lines in list.c' is false arithmetic (helper + two mismatch warnings ≈ +15-20 lines; sell it as authority reduction, not subtraction), and the closest pinned test — test_hold.sh:1893-1917 reads console_sock FROM THE RECORD (:1910) and asserts prune removes it (:1913) — stays green only because stored==computed for real calls; it must be re-run as the sentinel. Folding the play-2 idx fix into this commit is natural: unlink_log_index_for_log applied to the COMPUTED log path in both sites closes the FACT finding at list.c:701 without double-churning the same lines.
amendments: Compute the sock path via hold_format_console_sock_path (console.h:13) — the exact function every writer uses — instead of hand-rolling '%s/%s.sock'; runtime->console include direction is legal per scripts/lint_layers.sh. Compute the idx via hold_log_idx_path on the computed log.

Apply derivation in BOTH sites in this one commit (hold_prune_one_run :626-653 and sweep branch :699-707) and add the computed-idx unlink in the sweep branch here, closing ledger finding list.c:701 (FACT) — do not fix the idx against stored paths in the store commit and re-touch it here.

Mismatch warning: fire once per artifact per purge, stderr only ('hold: warning: record %s names a %s outside the store; left untouched: %s'), never stdout — docker-parity output discipline. When paths match (the universal case), zero new output.

Test fixture idiom: forge from a REAL ended call ('hold -d true', record_ended_soon, then sed log_path/console_sock in the record) — NOT a synthetic pid-12345 record, whose eval_state is nondeterministic when pid 12345 happens to be live on the test host.
SPEC.md gets exactly one added line under Safety invariants: 'Purge removes only store-resident artifacts derived from the call id; paths stored in records are never followed for deletion.'

NAMED FOLLOW-UPS (not this commit): (a) redial append still honors stored log_path (call.c ~:115-119 -> open_log_append_no_symlink) — the trust inversion is deliberately partial; (b) read-side consumers (hold logs/tail open r.log_path at signal.c:174/:845 with O_NOFOLLOW on the final component only) — same-privilege reads, lower severity, separate decision.

pinned_conflicts.what: PINS the leader-gone GROUP_LIVE fallback authorizing a signal with a recorded-but-uncompared token: 'hold -d bash -c "sleep 60 & exit 0"' must list as Up and 'hold end' must exit 0. Directly falsifies play 1's assumption #2 ('no existing test depends on the fallback succeeding'). The min-starttime-floor amendment keeps it green (sleep starts at/after the leader; a zombie leader's starttime equals the recorded value so strict-< passes); the finding's alternative remedy 'refuse leaderless groups without an override' is REJECTED by this pin.

Pins rc 2 plus stderr matching 'process group/session differs|cannot be signaled' for the live-leader tamper case. All new refusal messages introduced by play 1 must keep the 'cannot be signaled' phrasing to stay inside the message family this test establishes; it also constrains cleanup idiom (record .bak/restore, decoy teardown) for the new forged-record tests.
Pins rc 5 (not 2) for pgid<=1 tampering via the early guard at signal.c:96-99. The fail-closed rework must not reorder validation so this becomes rc 2.

Pins that bare 'hold prune' sweeps orphan .log files that have NO record and NO reserve. Play 2's reserve-skip must be conditional on reserve existence (and age), never a blanket behavior change to the orphan sweep.

Pins that bare 'hold prune' returns rc 0 AND removes a garbage record file (:661-662). Play 2's corrupt-removal warning/counting must not change the exit code or stop the removal; stderr is unredirected in this test so new warnings are safe, but the removal itself is load-bearing.

Reads console_sock out of the RECORD (:1910) and asserts targeted 'hold prune <id>' removes that exact path (:1913). Under play 3 the unlink target becomes the computed path — identical for real calls — so this test is the regression sentinel proving derivation is behavior-identical for hold-written records.

purge --force on a live saved call routes through hold_cmd_signal_action (list.c:930-937) and tolerates TERM->KILL timing with a 20x retry loop — but tolerates timing only, not refusals. Play 1 must introduce zero new refusal paths for healthy live calls or this test flakes into hard failure (rc 2 would leave the record present every attempt).

Fixtures still write 'requires_elevation' and 'alias' keys, confirming the ledger's store.md/projection-drift finding — that drift belongs to another cluster; this cluster's commits must NOT 'fix' the fixture or the projection in passing.

No test anywhere greps the purge summary strings ('nothing to purge', 'purged N past call'), no test pins the '.<id>.tmp' temp-name shape, and no test uses a TERM-trapping child — so the escalation branch signal.c:279-312 is currently unpinned entirely. dockerish :51-52 pins only that the .log.idx sidecar EXISTS for a live call, not its purge lifecycle.

implementation_risks: RANK 1 — Play 1 false refusal on adopted groups: a process older than the recorded leader can join the group via setpgid (pipeline/job-control edge in 'hold on' sessions), tripping the starttime floor and refusing a legitimate 'hold end' with rc 2. Mitigation: strict-< comparison (equal ticks pass), skip 'Z' members, refusal message names the cause; watch the adopted-shell tests (~test_hold.sh:3700s) and :1873 in CI. This is the one user-visible ACCESS regression candidate in the cluster.

RANK 2 — Play 2 reserve staleness threshold: too short re-opens the delete-nascent-call race for slow console/broker starts; too long delays post-crash cleanup by one sweep cycle. 600s is >100x the observed reserve->JSON window; document the constant beside STOP_TIMEOUT_MS-style config. A crashed start is otherwise a PERMANENT blind spot — the unamended sketch ships that bug.

RANK 3 — Play 2 unique temps convert concurrent record writers (rename/save at call.c:266/:335 vs reaper hold_mark_run_finished at record.c:1015) from loud EEXIST death/retry into silent last-write-wins. Data loss is unchanged (the loser's update was lost either way) but detectability regresses; the per-id flock is a named deferral and MUST be stated in the commit message.

RANK 4 — Play 1 macOS seam: hold_group_min_live_member_starttime needs a second KERN_PROC_ALL sysctl walk; starttime units differ per platform (Linux boot-relative ticks vs macOS usec wallclock, process.c:260-265) but are self-consistent within a boot; the boot-id gate at signal.c:107 only fires when r->has_boot — a boot-id-less record compared across boots yields garbage ticks, an exposure that already exists in the equality check at :117 and is not widened. Verify the macOS branch compiles zero-warning; CI is Linux-first.

RANK 5 — Play 1 changes the cross-uid recycled-pid outcome from misleading EPERM exit 3 to honest identity exit 2 (exe fail-closed path). No test pins exit 3 there (verified), but external scripts matching on 3 see a behavior change; changelog entry required.

RANK 6 — Shared-surface collision with other clusters: start.c (reserve/error paths), signal.c (validate), record.c (writers), broker/shell.c are all named surfaces in other clusters' likely changes. Land this cluster as commits 1->2->3 in one short window; commit 2 touches start.c NOT at all (reserve logic unchanged there) but shell.c adoption and record.c writers will conflict textually with any console-cluster work — rebase order matters.

RANK 7 — Play 3 migration stranding: records copied from an old HOME leave old-location files unswept (warned, never followed). Announced by the mismatch warning and consistent with no-legacy-shims; but the warning will repeat on every purge until the user removes the stale record — acceptable, documented.

RANK 8 — Purge summary/wording additions (corrupt counter, mismatch warnings) are unpinned today but become new de facto contracts the moment they ship; keep wording in the existing kept-string style (list.c:850-861) and pin the new strings in the new tests so future drift is caught.
revised_change_set:
Three commits on tempo-refinement, in order; each independently green (build zero-warning C11, lint_layers clean, full suite passing).

COMMIT 1 — signal: fail closed on unverifiable identity; SPEC states the kept guarantee
Files: src/platform/process.c, include/hold/platform.h, src/runtime/signal.c, docs/SPEC.md, tests/test_hold.sh.
(1) platform/process.c: add hold_group_min_live_member_starttime(pid_t pgid, pid_t sid, uint64_t *min_out) -> 0 with min of live (non-'Z') members' starttimes when >=1 live member found, -1 on scan error or zero live members. Linux branch: /proc walk reusing read_process_ids_state + hold_read_proc_stat_tokens; __APPLE__ branch: KERN_PROC_ALL walk using kp_proc.p_starttime usec (units match the mac branch of hold_read_proc_stat_tokens). Declare in include/hold/platform.h next to hold_group_session_liveness (:17).
(2) signal.c validate_signal_target, live-leader branch (:121-129): when r->proc_starttime_ticks==0 and exe tokens are the only identity, treat hold_read_proc_exe(...) != 0 as refusal — rc 2, message 'hold: error: call %s process identity is unverifiable and cannot be signaled'. Add #ifdef HOLD_TESTING guard: getenv("HOLD_TEST_FAIL_PROC_EXE") forces the failure path (guarded like layout.c:111, unlike the unguarded hooks at start.c:1406/:1441).
(3) signal.c leader-gone fallback (:150-157): keep the !have_identity_token refusal; then (a) starttime token present -> call hold_group_min_live_member_starttime; refuse rc 2 with the existing 'process identity differs...cannot be signaled' message when it returns 0 and min < r->proc_starttime_ticks, or returns -1; (b) starttime==0 (exe-only token) -> refuse rc 2 'identity is unverifiable and cannot be signaled'. No re-read before the kills at :263/:298 (rejected as theater); no state.c change (named deferral: state.c:54-59 exe fail-open).
(4) docs/SPEC.md:59-60 reword: 'Signals are delivered to the group only after the recorded identity (start time, exe identity) revalidates immediately before every delivery — a recycled PID is never knowingly signaled.'
(5) Tests: T1-T3 below; re-run :750 and :1873 as sentinels.
Deferred out of this commit (named): state.c fail-open; hold off HOLD_ON_PID starttime check (shell.c:784 finding, separate surface); stats/ports observation identity re-checks (observe-only findings).

COMMIT 2 — store: crash-safe temps; sweep honors two-phase creation everywhere it exists, and creation is two-phase everywhere
Files: src/store/record.c, src/runtime/shell.c, src/runtime/list.c, tests/test_hold.sh.
(1) record.c hold_write_record_atomic: replace fixed-name open at :156/:163 with hold_open_unique_temp(dir, r->id, 0600, tmp, sizeof(tmp)); hold_write_public_index_atomic at :397/:401 likewise with 0644, KEEPING the fchmod(fd,0644) at :405 and root fchown at :408. Failure-path unlink(tmp) at :385/:482 and rename-to-final/reserve-unlink (:362-365) unchanged.
(2) shell.c adopt path: after shell_hashed_adopt_id succeeds, create record_dir/.<id>.reserve (O_WRONLY|O_CREAT|O_EXCL|O_CLOEXEC, 0600 — mirror start.c:209) BEFORE the log open at :469 and sock creation at :481; add the reserve to shell_id_available (:318-340); unlink it on every adopt failure path (record.c:365 clears it on success).
(3) list.c sweep_logs (:748) and sweep_consoles (:784): before unlinking an orphan, build record_dir/.<id>.reserve; if it exists with mtime age < 600s, skip the artifact; if older, unlink the reserve and proceed. sweep_public unchanged (public entries are written only after the record JSON, start.c:1444 — no window).
(4) list.c corrupt-record branch (:681-688): stderr 'hold: warning: removing corrupt record %s' on both unlink branches; new stats->removed_corrupt counter; summary (:846-867) appends '; removed N corrupt record(s)' and prints 'nothing to purge' only when removed==0 && removed_corrupt==0. Do NOT touch stats->removed or the stdout purged-id lines (:711).
(5) list.c record-dir (and public-dir) sweep loops: collect dot-prefixed '*.tmp' entries with mtime older than 60s (catches both new unique-temp litter and legacy '.<id>.tmp' tombs).
(6) Tests: T4-T7 below. Commit message names the deferral: 'per-id flock for record load-modify-write (record.c:1015) deferred; unique temps make concurrent-writer clobbers silent where they were loud.'

COMMIT 3 — purge: deletion targets derived from (layout, id); record path strings never followed
Files: src/runtime/list.c, docs/SPEC.md, tests/test_hold.sh.
(1) list.c static helper compute_call_paths(const struct hold_store *store, const char *id, char *log, size_t log_n, char *sock, size_t sock_n): log via hold_checked_snprintf('%s/%s.log', store->log_dir, id); sock via hold_format_console_sock_path (add hold/console.h include — legal direction per lint_layers).
(2) hold_prune_one_run (:626-653): unlink computed idx (unlink_log_index_for_log on computed log), computed log, computed sock in place of r.log_path/r.console_sock; when r.has_log && strcmp(stored, computed)!=0 emit one stderr warning 'hold: warning: record %s names a log outside the store; left untouched: %s' (same for console_sock); record-file and public-index unlinks already computed, unchanged.
(3) Sweep record-branch (:699-707): identical substitution PLUS the computed-idx unlink — this closes the FACT finding at list.c:701 in the same edit.
(4) docs/SPEC.md Safety invariants: add 'Purge removes only store-resident artifacts derived from the call id; paths stored in records are never followed for deletion.'
(5) Tests: T8-T10 below. Named follow-ups in the commit message: redial append (call.c:115-119) and read-side log_path consumers keep stored-path trust for now.
test_plan: T1 (new, commit 1) test_stop_escalates_past_term_ignoring_group: id=$("$HOLD_BIN" -d sh -c 'trap "" TERM; sleep 300' | extract_id); pgid=$(record_pgid "$id"); "$HOLD_BIN" stop "$id" -> rc 0; pgid_terminated "$pgid". First-ever pin of the TERM->KILL escalation branch (signal.c:279-312) and of the post-TERM revalidation not falsely refusing survivors; runs ~5-6s (STOP_TIMEOUT_MS=5000) — comment the duration, stays under HOLD_TEST_TIMEOUT=25.

T2 (new, commit 1) test_signal_refuses_forged_leader_gone_group: decoy=$("$HOLD_BIN" -d sleep 300 | extract_id); donor=$("$HOLD_BIN" -d true | extract_id); record_ended_soon "$donor"; sed the donor record (cp .bak first, idiom of :773-784): pgid/sid -> decoy's, pid unchanged (dead), proc_starttime_ticks -> decoy's value + 1000000, state -> running; set +e; "$HOLD_BIN" stop "$donor" 2>err; rc=$?; set -e; assert rc 2, err matches 'cannot be signaled', decoy still 'Up' in list; cleanup: restore .bak, stop decoy. Proves the starttime floor refuses a foreign older group.

T3 (new, commit 1) test_signal_refuses_unverifiable_exe_identity: decoy=$("$HOLD_BIN" -d sleep 300 | extract_id); sed decoy's own record: proc_starttime_ticks -> 0 (leave exe_dev/exe_ino); HOLD_TEST_FAIL_PROC_EXE=1 "$HOLD_BIN" stop "$decoy" -> rc 2, err 'unverifiable...cannot be signaled', decoy's sleep still alive (kill -0 its pgid); then unset hook, restore record, stop decoy for real -> rc 0. Pins fail-closed exe and proves the hook is inert without HOLD_TESTING... (hook only compiled under -DHOLD_TESTING, Makefile:37).

T4 (new, commit 2) test_stale_record_tmp_does_not_block_rewrites: id=$("$HOLD_BIN" -d sleep 1 | extract_id); : > "$HOME/.local/state/hold/.$id.tmp"; sleep 2; grep '"state": "exited"' record (reaper succeeded despite tomb — today this exhausts silently); "$HOLD_BIN" rename "$id" fresh -> rc 0 (today: fatal 'File exists'); assert .$id.tmp still present (collected later by T6's litter rule, not by the writer).

T5 (new, commit 2) test_purge_sweep_respects_reserve_marker: ensure_user_fixture_store; fid=64-hex fixture id; : > "$store/.$fid.reserve"; : > "$store/$fid.log"; mkdir -p console; : > "$store/console/$fid.sock"; "$HOLD_BIN" purge -a >/dev/null 2>&1; assert log and sock SURVIVE (today: deleted); touch -d '1 hour ago' "$store/.$fid.reserve"; "$HOLD_BIN" purge -a; assert reserve, log, sock all gone.

T6 (new, commit 2) test_purge_sweep_collects_stale_tmp_litter: plant "$store/.$fid.tmp" and a unique-shaped "$store/.$fid.12345.deadbeefdeadbeef.tmp", touch -d '5 minutes ago' both; purge -> both gone; plant a fresh one (current mtime); purge -> survives.
